title: The Surprise Party

jokes.text: So you're Mocha Joe's mom. / Yeah, yeah. My name's Jane. / Mocha Jane.
jokes.setup: Larry meets the mother of his coffee shop rival
jokes.punchline: She's adopted the same 'Mocha' branding as her son
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that establishes family branding absurdity with perfect economy

jokes.text: You're Latte Larry! / Oh, I should have known. / We're gonna take you down, Latte Larry.
jokes.setup: Jane realizes who Larry is
jokes.punchline: Immediate declaration of war using his coffee shop branding
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from recognition to threat, using the branded name comedically

jokes.text: We shall see, Mocha Jane. We shall see.
jokes.setup: Jane threatens Larry with business competition
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly formal, theatrical response to coffee shop rivalry
jokes.explanation: Larry's characteristically pompous response to trivial conflict

jokes.text: I wanted to see, uh, the Colosseum before the gladiators started fighting.
jokes.setup: Larry visits his coffee shop before it opens
jokes.punchline: Grandiose historical metaphor for coffee shop competition
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that captures Larry's tendency to dramatize mundane situations

jokes.text: Who's coming in? Howie Mandel and his family every day?
jokes.setup: Freddy mentions Purell at every table
jokes.punchline: Larry references the famously germaphobic comedian as the only customer for such measures
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that earns its laugh through specificity and character knowledge

jokes.text: I feel like I'm with Howard Hughes right before the nails go big.
jokes.setup: Larry shows off his elaborate urinal system
jokes.punchline: Freddy compares Larry's obsessive bathroom innovations to famous recluse's descent into madness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical reference that perfectly captures the absurdity of Larry's bathroom obsession

jokes.text: Let 'em go home. / Then you lose customers. / Who cares? I'd rather have a no-coffee buyer than a defecator.
jokes.setup: Freddy asks about customers who need to defecate
jokes.punchline: Larry prefers losing sales to allowing bathroom use
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry logic - prioritizing cleanliness over basic business sense with perfect character consistency

jokes.text: They have pee envy, actually.
jokes.setup: Larry explains why women would want his squat toilet
jokes.punchline: Claims women are envious of men's urination method
jokes.explanation: Ridiculous pseudo-psychology delivered with Larry's characteristic confidence

jokes.text: It's like a Pilates class, though. Is it hard?
jokes.setup: Larry demonstrates the women's squat toilet
jokes.punchline: Freddy recognizes it as an exercise position rather than a bathroom solution
jokes.explanation: Good observation that highlights the impracticality of Larry's invention

jokes.text: My legs are killing me. / I gotta get up.
jokes.setup: Both men try the squat toilet
jokes.punchline: They can't maintain the position, proving its impracticality
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that demonstrates the absurdity of Larry's design through experience

jokes.text: Marconi used to go mountain climbing naked.
jokes.setup: Larry says inventors are supposed to be eccentric
jokes.punchline: Bizarre historical 'fact' about the radio inventor
jokes.explanation: Random absurd 'fact' that's perfectly delivered but somewhat disconnected

jokes.text: People hate surprise parties. / You, maybe. Everybody I know loves a surprise party. / No. Everybody.
jokes.setup: Susie wants to throw Jeff a surprise party
jokes.punchline: Larry insists everyone hates them while Susie insists everyone loves them
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry vs. normal person disagreement with characteristic absolute positions

jokes.text: The guy's got a heart condition. / Surprises are dangerous! / He'll be happy? He'll be dead.
jokes.setup: Larry argues against the surprise party
jokes.punchline: Escalates from concern to predicting Jeff's death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that sets up the episode's central premise with typical Larry catastrophizing

jokes.text: Andrew 'Rusty' Holzer? / Rusty, in quotes? / He put a nickname on the card? Why's he doing that?
jokes.setup: Larry sees the doctor's business card
jokes.punchline: Confusion and judgment about self-imposed nickname in quotes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry nitpicking of social conventions with good attention to detail

jokes.text: I guess you gotta be a little careful of the food you bring in because sometimes it has an odor, and it... it smells up the office. You know, like on a plane. You bring food on a plane?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to make conversation with receptionist
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-explanation about food odors leading to personal questions
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social awkwardness - overthinking simple interactions into uncomfortable territory

jokes.text: Oh, I know that face. That's, 'Oh, you're coming onto me.' / I'm not coming onto you. I'm not hitting on you. Everything's not a hit.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about artwork in the office
jokes.punchline: Receptionist thinks he's hitting on her, Larry launches into defensive explanation
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Larry's innocent question misinterpreted, leading to over-explanation

jokes.text: Who gives themselves a nickname? / Most people are given nicknames for some personality quirk or trait. If you ate a lot of peaches one day, when you were a kid and, and you threw up, maybe you'd be called 'Peaches' for the rest of your life.
jokes.setup: Rusty explains he's trying out the nickname
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate theory about how nicknames are earned, with specific example
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry lecture on social conventions with vivid, absurd example

jokes.text: Where, where'd you come up with 'Rusty'? / I feel like a Rusty. / Good enough.
jokes.setup: Larry demands explanation for the nickname choice
jokes.punchline: Rusty's non-explanation and Larry's resigned acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the nickname setup with Larry's characteristic resignation to absurdity

jokes.text: Chris Martin's gonna, he's gonna sing. / Chris Martin? / Wow. / You like Coldplay? / Cold... Oh, I love... I mean that, that's big. / They're great. Yeah. / That's a lot of pressure.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions Chris Martin will perform at the party
jokes.punchline: Rusty's excitement escalates from interest to feeling pressure about attending
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing how celebrity names affect people, building to Rusty wanting an invite

jokes.text: I calculated your speed, my speed, the distance, my average urination time, and according to my calculations, I would have had to wait three to four minutes, whereas you only had to wait 12 to 15 seconds.
jokes.setup: Wally asks why Larry passed him in the hallway
jokes.punchline: Larry's mathematical justification for cutting in line at the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - applying mathematical precision to justify rude behavior with perfect character consistency

jokes.text: You should have given me one of these. / You know? / No, you've gotta wait. / You're blocking traffic. You're in the slow car. You've gotta let the fast cars through.
jokes.setup: Larry argues about hallway etiquette
jokes.punchline: Applies driving rules to walking with a disabled person
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - treating human interaction like traffic laws, especially insensitive given Wally's disability

jokes.text: I think you have bad walker etiquette. You're a hallway hog.
jokes.setup: Argument about who was wrong in the hallway
jokes.punchline: Larry coins 'hallway hog' to blame the disabled person
jokes.explanation: Perfect neologism that captures Larry's ability to blame anyone but himself

jokes.text: I've dated two women in, in wheelchairs. / Whoa. Two? / Yeah. / My disabled bona fides are well-established. / She caught me cheating with the other... with the other disabled woman.
jokes.setup: Larry establishes his credentials with disabled people
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was cheating between two disabled women
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation that reveals Larry's capacity for awful behavior while trying to prove he's not prejudiced

jokes.text: He is so transparent. He's trying to finagle an invitation to the party. / We had two dinners with him and his wife. Aggressively boring, fuckin' brutal.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests inviting the cardiologist
jokes.punchline: Susie sees through it and describes the doctor as 'aggressively boring'
jokes.explanation: 'Aggressively boring' is a perfect phrase that captures the active nature of tedious people

jokes.text: You're trying to kill him. / Trying to kill who? / Jeff. You want to give him a heart attack, and you want him to die at that party. That's your plan, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Larry continues arguing against the surprise party
jokes.punchline: Directly accuses Susie of attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Larry's paranoia into direct murder accusation - peak Larry logic

jokes.text: You ruin this surprise party, I'm gonna fuck you up.
jokes.setup: Larry threatens to go to the cops about the 'murder plot'
jokes.punchline: Susie's direct threat of violence over a surprise party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie response - immediate escalation to violence over party planning

jokes.text: It's as if coffee is dictating to us when it must be consumed. I'm not okay with that.
jokes.setup: Boris explains his heated cup invention
jokes.punchline: Frames cold coffee as an oppressive force controlling human behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant personification of coffee as tyrannical - perfectly captures inventor mindset

jokes.text: You named your dog... Adolf? / I named him after my grandfather, my opa. He kind of looks like him. He's got a handsome face, but you can tell... he's stern.
jokes.setup: Larry notices the dog's name
jokes.punchline: Boris explains it's after his grandfather, describing Hitler-like features
jokes.explanation: Peak uncomfortable comedy - innocent explanation that makes everything worse

jokes.text: Heil Hitler! / Hey! Good boy. Yeah. Good boy.
jokes.setup: Dog growls at Larry then suddenly obeys
jokes.punchline: Larry accidentally discovers the dog responds to Nazi salute
jokes.explanation: Shock comedy masterpiece - Larry accidentally discovers the horrible truth through desperation

jokes.text: When I trained him, I would put a treat out with my arm up, like that. Heel.
jokes.setup: Larry explains what happened with the Nazi salute
jokes.punchline: Boris provides innocent explanation while demonstrating identical gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - provides innocent explanation while making it look exactly the same

jokes.text: He's a white supremacist. He named his dog Adolf. / He named his dog after his grandfather. / Hey, if my grandfather was named Pol Pot, I wouldn't name my dog Pol Pot.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Leon about Boris
jokes.punchline: Larry's logic about not naming pets after genocidal dictators
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - extends the principle to other dictators to prove his point

jokes.text: He's part of a tornado-chasing troop called Stormfront.
jokes.setup: Larry continues listing suspicious things about Boris
jokes.punchline: Names that sound Nazi-adjacent for tornado chasing
jokes.explanation: Clever name that could be innocent weather terminology or Nazi reference

jokes.text: No black man would ever chase a fuckin' tornado. / Yeah, that, that's so true. / That shit is dumb as fuck.
jokes.setup: Discussion of tornado chasing shows
jokes.punchline: Leon's racial observation about dangerous hobbies
jokes.explanation: Peak Leon - cutting through pretense with practical racial humor

jokes.text: Pussy tea. Huh? / All the taste of pussy, without the commitment. / Like, put that shit in a tea bag.
jokes.setup: Leon pitches invention ideas
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate product concept delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Outrageous Leon idea delivered with perfect entrepreneurial confidence

jokes.text: I feel like Susie wants to give this guy a heart attack with all these people yelling, 'Surprise.' She wants to kill him. / You're saying the point of the surprise party is to kill him?
jokes.setup: Larry explains his Susie theory
jokes.punchline: Leon clarifies Larry's murder conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback that restates the central absurd premise

jokes.text: I was watching Columbo one night, at three in the morning. This motherfucker had me convinced I did the shit.
jokes.setup: Leon talks about loving mysteries
jokes.punchline: Claims Columbo convinced him he committed the murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon observation about TV's psychological power, delivered with his unique perspective

jokes.text: If you tell him... She'll kill me. / But then, if you don't tell him... He could have a heart attack and die. / So what are you gonna do? / Oh, I'm not gonna tell him. / Then you'll have to live with that. / Eh.
jokes.setup: Leon analyzes Larry's moral dilemma
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately chooses self-preservation with casual 'Eh'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moral cowardice - faced with life or death choice, picks convenience

jokes.text: I'm just between appointments. I needed somewhere to wait, and this is a waiting room. / You're just using this waiting room... To wait. / That's not done.
jokes.setup: Larry sits in doctor's office waiting room without appointment
jokes.punchline: Defends using waiting room for its literal purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - taking room's name literally to justify inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: You go in a restaurant, you see nobody in there, you don't think it's any good. When you see people there, you think it's good. Same thing with the doctor's office.
jokes.setup: Receptionist says he can't just wait there
jokes.punchline: Larry argues his presence helps business through social proof
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - applying restaurant psychology to justify inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: You're living in this paranoid world of hitting and stalking, and... 'Everybody wants me.' There's a certain kind of conceit attached to this.
jokes.setup: Receptionist accuses Larry of stalking
jokes.punchline: Larry accuses her of narcissistic paranoia
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry counter-attack - turning accusation back on accuser's vanity

jokes.text: You call Jeff, you ask him out for dinner, at Scopa, on the 20th. / But you never got the text. / No, I'd get the text. / No. You never got the text.
jokes.setup: Larry explains scheme to crash the party
jokes.punchline: Insists Rusty deny receiving a text that hasn't been sent yet
jokes.explanation: Good Larry scheming with characteristic insistence on details

jokes.text: Stop being such a wuss. / I don't know... / Rusty! / I'll do it. / Proud of you.
jokes.setup: Rusty hesitates about lying to Susie
jokes.punchline: Larry uses the nickname to inspire courage, Rusty responds
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the nickname with Larry playing motivational coach

jokes.text: Susie scares me. / Yeah, she scares everybody, but you-you can't let her run your life. / She's very scary. I'm scared of Susie.
jokes.setup: Doctor hesitant to lie to Susie
jokes.punchline: His repeated confession of fear despite being a grown man
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing universal fear of Susie. The repetition adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I'd kill for an amputee friend.
jokes.setup: Wally mentions having an amputee friend
jokes.punchline: Larry's envious response about collecting disabled friends
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Larry treating disabled friends like collectibles

jokes.text: I don't even know how you ever got a license. / Oh, it was pretty easy. I mean, I studied up for it. And I went to the DMV, and I signed up, went home, waited for my appointment... / I don't need all the details of that particular day in your life.
jokes.setup: Larry insults Wally's driving, Wally starts explaining
jokes.punchline: Wally launches into unnecessary detail about getting his license
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Wally's literal-minded response to rhetorical insult

jokes.text: You just said you would spare me the details, but you're not sparing. / I'm skipping over a lot of stuff.
jokes.setup: Wally continues with details after promising to spare them
jokes.punchline: Claims he's actually skipping a lot
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic - Wally believes he's being concise while being incredibly detailed

jokes.text: You can park anywhere with one of these. / Oh. Love to have one of those.
jokes.setup: Wally uses handicapped spot
jokes.punchline: Larry openly covets disability parking privileges
jokes.explanation: Larry's naked self-interest about disability benefits

jokes.text: 'Cause it fucking sucks here right now. Look at this... weather, this rain, it's so oppressive. / Bahamas! Here I come, baby! I've been waiting so long for this. I can't wait to get the fuck out of here.
jokes.setup: Susie books a trip to the Bahamas
jokes.punchline: Excessive enthusiasm about escaping, suggesting murder getaway
jokes.explanation: Susie's over-enthusiasm feeds into Larry's conspiracy theory

jokes.text: Jeff hates the Caribbean. / Soon. / There you go.
jokes.setup: Wally reports Susie wants to go to Bahamas soon
jokes.punchline: Larry sees this as evidence since Jeff hates the Caribbean
jokes.explanation: Larry's detective work finding 'evidence' in innocent travel plans

jokes.text: The number is G921... / Yeah, you don't have to read the number. I see it. / But if they ask you. / If they ask me, yeah, I'll read it out there. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Wally lends Larry his handicapped placard
jokes.punchline: Over-explains obvious details about using it
jokes.explanation: Typical Wally over-explanation of simple concepts

jokes.text: Hit the fuckin' lottery. The fuckin' handicapped lottery, Larry. / The sky's the motherfuckin' limit.
jokes.setup: Larry gets the handicapped placard
jokes.punchline: Leon celebrates disability fraud as hitting the lottery
jokes.explanation: Peak Leon - celebrating fraud with infectious enthusiasm

jokes.text: Is that a hobbit? / No way! / Aah, to hell with you.
jokes.setup: Jane confronts Larry about parking illegally again
jokes.punchline: Leon's reaction to seeing Jane, her dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Leon's blunt observation and Jane's matter-of-fact dismissal

jokes.text: You know what? I just might take a lead pipe to your leg so we can get one of these all the time.
jokes.setup: Leon enjoys the benefits of the handicapped placard
jokes.punchline: Suggests permanently disabling Larry for parking privileges
jokes.explanation: Hilariously dark Leon logic - casual violence for convenience

jokes.text: Heil Hitler! / Yes, that's a good boy. / Who's a good Adolf? Ooh!
jokes.setup: Leon uses Larry's knowledge about the dog
jokes.punchline: Successfully commands Adolf with Nazi salute
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with Leon enthusiastically using Larry's discovery

jokes.text: Adolf! / Was this important?
jokes.setup: Adolf destroys the heated cup prototype
jokes.punchline: Boris's understated reaction to his invention being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to disaster - Boris's Germanic understatement

jokes.text: The dog ate it. / What dog? / Adolf? / So this is the best excuse you can come up with? / That's what Adolf wanted to eat is... plastic? / Well, he wanted to eat the Star of David, which was right in front of the placard.
jokes.setup: Larry has to explain the destroyed placard
jokes.punchline: Claims the Nazi dog specifically targeted the Jewish symbol
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the Nazi dog's motivation makes the story worse

jokes.text: Well, he wanted to eat the Star of David, which was right in front of the placard. The placard was collateral damage.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining Adolf's motivation for eating the placard
jokes.punchline: His theory about the dog's anti-Semitic appetite
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the Adolf subplot. The 'collateral damage' phrase makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Why are you going in a circle? You could have just walked backwards. / Easier to go down stairs this way.
jokes.setup: Wally does a three-point turn with his walker on stairs
jokes.punchline: Explains inefficient method as easier
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy about walker logistics

jokes.text: When have I ever said that about Susie?
jokes.setup: Jeff says Susie has been 'a doll'
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges this is completely out of character for him to say
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment about how unusual it is for Jeff to praise Susie

jokes.text: Because he has a heart condition. And this could be a matter of life and death. / Why can't I just call him? / Because he has no cell phone. He lost it.
jokes.setup: Wally's logical questions about Larry's plan
jokes.punchline: Larry's convenient excuses that make his plan the only option
jokes.explanation: Good plot mechanics joke. Larry's explanations perfectly set up the inevitable failure.

jokes.text: I don't know if I have the right clothes for it. Should I wear a, a jack...? / It doesn't matter. You're not going to the party. You're just waiting outside and telling him about it.
jokes.setup: Wally worrying about party attire
jokes.punchline: Larry's impatient clarification that Wally isn't actually attending
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Wally's social anxiety vs. Larry's task-focused impatience.

jokes.text: Hey, you can't park here. It's handicapped. / Sir, I have a walker. I can park here. / Where's your placard? / I usually have one. It's just, a friend of mine borrowed it, and... / No, no, no. If you don't have one now, you can't park here.
jokes.setup: Wally trying to park in handicapped spot without placard
jokes.punchline: The actual disabled person being denied parking privileges due to bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - the one person who actually needs handicapped parking can't use it. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Hey, you can't park here. It's handicapped. / Sir, I have a walker. I can park here. / Where's your placard? / I usually have one. It's just, a friend of mine borrowed it, and... / No, no, no. If you don't have one now, you can't park here. Come on. Now. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Wally parks in handicapped spot without placard
jokes.punchline: Actually disabled person gets ticketed while Larry's inside committing fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic justice - legitimate user punished while fraud succeeds

jokes.text: I'll probably leave before you get on. / I mean, people pay a lot of money to see us. / Yeah, I know. I don't like to stay around too long for stuff like that.
jokes.setup: Chris Martin inviting Larry to stay for his performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual dismissal of the star's expensive entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry social awkwardness - insulting celebrities to their face. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Why would you want to shower with another person? The scents, and the aromas, and the... / Must be hard to get temperature consensus in a shower.
jokes.setup: Chris mentions singing in the shower with company
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical objections to shared showers
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - finding practical problems with romantic activities

jokes.text: You sing in the shower? / If I have company, I'll sing in the shower, yeah. / Why would you want to shower with another person?
jokes.setup: Chris Martin mentioning singing in the shower with company
jokes.punchline: Larry's confused questioning of romantic shower sharing
jokes.explanation: Good Larry social obliviousness. His practical objections to romance are perfectly character-driven.

jokes.text: You don't, with respect, look like a Rusty.
jokes.setup: Rusty introduces himself to Chris Martin
jokes.punchline: Chris immediately calls out the artificial nickname
jokes.explanation: Good callback with outsider immediately seeing through the fake nickname

jokes.text: Oh! Uh, uh... uh, sir! Uh, Jeff? Don't go in there! Uh, don't... Wait. Don't go in there. It's a... It's a... / Surprise!
jokes.setup: Wally trying to warn Jeff about the surprise party
jokes.punchline: His failure to get the words out before the surprise happens
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing failure. The plan falls apart at exactly the wrong moment. Good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Surprise! / Wow. / What? / Too bad.
jokes.setup: Jeff walks into surprise party
jokes.punchline: Larry's disappointed reaction that Jeff didn't have a heart attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry reaction - disappointed his friend didn't die proves his point

jokes.text: We left you a message. / I didn't get the message. / You're a liar! / Oh, no. I... Ow. Ow. / You are a liar! / You're a liar. / Ow. Ow. Oh... / You're a liar! / Oh! Oh! / Heart attack! / My heart. / Rusty! Rusty!
jokes.setup: Rusty claims he never got the cancellation message
jokes.punchline: Susie's violent reaction triggers the heart attack Larry predicted
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax - Larry's scheme backfires but his original prediction comes true anyway

jokes.text: Heart attack! / My heart. / Rusty!
jokes.setup: The surprise party shock and confrontation stress
jokes.punchline: Jeff actually having the heart attack Larry predicted
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark payoff to Larry's paranoia. The fact that he was right makes it funnier and darker. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: I really like those on the coffin. / I'mma take a quick pic. / No, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry looks at Jeff's funeral arrangements
jokes.punchline: Wants to photograph coffin handles for his coffee shop
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry behavior - finding business opportunities at his friend's funeral

jokes.text: You wanna work here part-time? / I do. / Ho-ho, well, most of the people who come in here are gonna be vile, vulgar, the dregs of society.
jokes.setup: Funeral director offering Larry a job
jokes.punchline: His description perfectly matching Larry himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - the funeral director unknowingly describing Larry perfectly. Great ending note.

jokes.text: How did I wind up here?! / Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Susie realizes she's at the funeral home
jokes.punchline: Her shocked realization about the consequences of her actions
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat showing Susie's horror at the situation she created

tmdb_overview: When Susie plans a surprise party for Jeff, Larry suspects she has ulterior motives, and enlists the help of his new friend Wally to ensure things don’t go awry. Larry connects with a problematic inventor to develop an innovative new product. Dr. Andrew "Rusty" Holzer, portrayed by Greg Germann, adopts the nickname "Rusty," a choice Larry openly opposes, echoing his disdain for the new waiting room decor and receptionist misinterpretation. Rusty highlights the necessity of professional supervision for the surprise party, especially during Chris Martin's performance, to ensure Jeff's safety. Larry's suspicions extend to Rusty's motives and Susie's intentions, prompting him to devise a subtle plan involving Rusty. Despite logistical challenges, Rusty's unfortunate encounter with Susie at the party leads to a distressing outcome.
